cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user provided a passage about Chinese students visiting the Yellow River Station in Norway's Arctic and is asking which statement about Wu Jinyou is true. First, I need to identify where in the text Wu Jinyou is mentioned. Let me scan through the passage again. Here's the relevant part: "Wu Jinyou, head of the Polar Research Institute of China, and the group's leader, said, 'I hope the students have not only a field trip to the Yellow River station, but are also touched by the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers here. I also expect in the future, some of them will choose to devote themselves to polar research and maybe even work with us.'" So, Wu Jinyou is the head of the Polar Research Institute of China and the leader of the group. His statement expresses that he hopes the students are inspired by the researchers' devotion and spirit and that some might pursue polar research. Now, looking at the options: (A) He is a graduate from Dalian Marinetime University. - The text mentions Zheng Li is a student from Dalian Marinetime University. There's no mention of Wu Jinyou's education here. So this is likely false. (B) He is the president of Dalian Marinetime University. - Again, no info about his position at Dalian Marinetime University. The text says he's head of the Polar Research Institute. So probably not B.
